primary_sources: Texas Rural Broadband Association (TRBA/TSTCI) member companies — txrba.com
Hill Country Portal utility services directory — hillcountryportal.com
Preseem Wireless ISP Texas Road Trip 2020 — preseem.com
Texas Co-op Power broadband article — texascooppower.com
BroadbandNow provider profiles (corroborating service area + tech)
source_summary.verification_approach: Cross-referenced TTA / TRBA membership against PUC ILEC directory plus direct provider website verification. WISPs verified against multiple independent sources (Preseem, BroadbandNow, provider site). All listed entities are explicitly non-major-carrier and operate independent infrastructure in Texas.

key_observations: Texas has a very deep rural cooperative ILEC base — 25+ member-owned co-ops, most founded 1949-1956 during REA buildout. These are prime acquisition targets if board considers consolidation (rare but happens — Hilliary acquired Lipan TC in 2024).

Electric cooperative broadband subsidiaries (GVEC, Bandera, PEC, United Cooperative Services) are a distinct sub-category. These are typically NOT for sale (board-controlled by electric co-op members) but the operations may have spinoff potential.
Independent WISPs (~25 in list) are the most likely acquisition targets — founder-owned, 10-20 year tenure, 1k-10k subscribers, owners increasingly aging or fatigued by capex demands.
Hilliary Communications is consolidating in the region (acquired Lipan TC 2024) so it's a strategic, not a target.
Nextlink (Round Rock HQ) is a large rural fixed wireless + fiber player with private equity backing — strategic acquirer not a target.
VTX1 (Raymondville) acquired Bee Creek Communications in 2024 — verify Bee Creek's standalone status; may already be subsidiary.
exclusions_applied: Hamilton County Telephone Co-op (hctc.net domain conflicts with Hill Country; TTA listing appears erroneous — actual Hamilton-County-TX entity not separately verified)
NCTC (North Central Telephone Cooperative — Tennessee/Kentucky, not Texas)
TDS Telecom (large regional, divesting TX operations to BBT and Hilliary)
Vyve Broadband (operates in TX but PE-backed multi-state cable consolidator, not independent local)
Wisper ISP (operates in 6 states — Texas NOT among them per current verification)
GTC Broadband (Missouri-based — distinct from any Texas Granbury / Glen Rose provider)
